title: refactor(release): share PR context resolution
summary: This refactor eliminates duplicate env/git resolution across `explore`, `resolvePullRequest`, and `resolvePrNumber` by introducing a shared `ResolvedGitHubContext` / `ResolvedPullRequestContext` data model and composing all downstream effects from a single resolved context. The motivation is sound, the layering is clean, and the Effect composition patterns are correct. Three issues worth noting: one test-coverage gap for a new exported helper, one silent error-message degradation in `pr-preview`, and a public API surface that is larger than what consumers strictly need.

findings.title: Branch-matching path of `resolvePullRequestFromContext` has no test coverage
findings.body: The new `resolvePullRequestFromContext` test only exercises the `explicitPrNumber !== null` branch (explicit PR number). The other branch — falling back to `selectConnectedPullRequest(context.branch, pullRequests)` when `explicitPrNumber` is `null` — has no focused coverage. Because this is a newly-extracted public helper, the happy path of branch matching should also have a test case, particularly since `selectConnectedPullRequest` already has its own tests and this exercises the routing logic between the two paths.

findings.title: `pr-preview.ts` drops a targeted error message when GitHub target cannot be resolved
`exploreFromContext` is exported publicly but is primarily an internal composition helper
findings.body: Four new symbols are added to the public barrel (`exploreFromContext`, `resolveGitHubContext`, `resolvePullRequestContext`, `resolvePullRequestFromContext`) plus two new types. `exploreFromContext` in particular is a split of the existing `explore()` for internal composition — it requires callers to already hold a `ResolvedGitHubContext` (a type that is itself now public), which creates an implicit dependency on call-site discipline that the original `explore()` did not impose. If external consumers call `exploreFromContext` with a stale or synthetic context, they can get a `Recon` snapshot where `branch`, `target`, and `token` disagree with what the git/env services would have returned. Consider whether `exploreFromContext` should stay internal (`liveGithubLayerForContext` correctly stayed private as a model for this). The `resolve*` helpers are more defensible as public API since they are the core reusable units.

residual_risks: No test for `resolvePullRequestContext()` itself (the combined helper) — the individual composing pieces are tested but not the integrated path that writes both PR resolution and context into `ResolvedPullRequestContext`.

The three `resolvePr*` variants (`resolvePullRequest`, `resolvePullRequestContext`, `resolvePullRequestFromContext`) and the legacy `resolvePrNumber` form a surface where the behavioral differences may not be obvious to future maintainers; a short JSDoc on each distinguishing its use case would reduce confusion.
